title: BookNest
overview: BookNest is an adaptive e-learning platform that connects readers with curated micro-courses, study communities, and personal progress tracking. Designed mobile-first for busy learners on the go.
challenge: Most e-learning apps felt like homework. Users abandoned courses mid-way because they couldn't see meaningful progress, felt isolated, and had no way to apply what they'd learned in context.
solution: We introduced a 'learning loop' model: bite-sized lessons, community challenges, and a progress visualisation that made every step feel rewarding. Social features turned solitary study into a shared experience.
research: Surveyed 200 online learners about drop-off triggers
Analysed completion rates across 10 popular e-learning platforms
Ran 3 rounds of moderated usability testing on mobile prototypes
Co-design sessions with a panel of 8 learning coaches

process.description: Identified three key drop-off moments: lesson 3, week 2, and the first 'hard' quiz. All three became design priorities.
Designed a streak system, achievement badges, and a weekly community challenge to sustain motivation.
Built a Framer prototype optimised for thumb reach and one-handed use — tested on actual commuters.
Shipped v1 with 40 course partners; achieved 10k active learners in month one.

results: Course completion rate up 55% vs. industry average
4.8 App Store rating within first month
Community features drove 30% of new sign-ups through referrals
